name: uproot (ROOT File I/O in Pure Python)

performance.notes: Pure Python ROOT file reading; faster than PyROOT for batch analysis

agent_readiness.notes: Pure Python ROOT file reading without ROOT C++ installation. import uproot. file = uproot.open('data.root'). tree = file['Events']. tree['Jet_pt'].array(). Awkward Array output by default. Streaming: tree.iterate(step_size='100 MB'). Async I/O with aiohttp for remote files (XRootD). Write ROOT files: uproot.recreate('out.root'). No CERN ROOT installation required. Used extensively in particle physics analysis. Parquet export via awkward-array.

gotchas: Only useful in particle physics / scientific computing contexts
Not all ROOT file formats supported (TH* histograms fully, TTree main focus)
Write support more limited than read support
Awkward Array output requires learning that API
best_when: Particle physics ROOT file analysis without CERN ROOT installation
Converting ROOT TTree data to NumPy/pandas/Arrow
Reading ROOT files in pure Python environments (no C++)
avoid_when: Non-HEP use cases (ROOT format specific to physics)
Writing complex ROOT histograms (use CERN ROOT directly)
summary: uproot reads and writes CERN ROOT files in pure Python without ROOT C++ installation. uproot.open('data.root')['Events']['Jet_pt'].array() returns awkward arrays. Streaming via iterate(). Async remote file I/O. Essential for particle physics analysis in Python. Converts ROOT TTrees to NumPy/pandas/Arrow. No CERN ROOT dependency — pure Python/NumPy/awkward.
